title: Electromechanical Systems
summary: This page covers electromechanical systems, specifically an armature-controlled DC motor driven by a current source. It derives the differential equation relating output angle to input torque for a rotational mechanical system, and extends this to a motor-driven load incorporating viscous damping and moment of inertia.

figures.caption: Rotational system for Drill 1.19 showing (a) a physical system with moment of inertia J, torsional spring K, and damper B subject to applied torque T(t), and (b) its free-body diagram with restoring torques Kθ(t) and Bθ̇(t).
figures.description: The figure has two side-by-side subfigures labeled (a) and (b) for a rotational mechanical system, with no Cartesian axes. In (a), a rigid rotor with moment of inertia J is connected to ground through a torsional spring K and damping B, with angular displacement labeled θ(t) and an applied torque T(t) shown at the shaft. In (b), the free-body diagram isolates the inertia J and shows the opposing restoring torques Kθ(t) and Bθ̇(t) acting against the applied torque T(t). Students should notice the analogy to a mass-spring-damper system and that spring and damper torques resist the rotation.
